Mathematical Type: Hopf algebra (antipode/link), Grafting operator  $B_{+}$ , Binary Operator, or Magma
Recursive Operation: Grafting, Coproduct, Binary set formation  $M(\alpha, \beta) = \{\alpha, \beta\}$ , or Concatenation
Linguistic Description: An operation that takes two distinct, independent syntactic objects from the workspace and combines them into a single new object to form argument structure or complex expressions.
Workspace Size Effect: Reduces workspace size by 1 (e.g.,  $n$  to  $n-1$ ) or increases total vertices/accessible terms.

Mathematical Type: Hopf algebra (link), Morphism reflection, Endomorphism, or Involutive Map
Recursive Operation: Grafting/Coproduct (with trace contraction/copying), Fold, or Re-merging a sub-constituent
Linguistic Description: An operation where an accessible term already within a syntactic object is merged with the root of that same object, creating a movement chain or copy while leaving a trace.
Accessible Terms Count: $n + 1$ ,  $1$  existing term, or remains the same ( $0$  change)
Workspace Size Effect: Zero net change; maintains constant workspace size (e.g.,  $n$  to  $n$ ).

Mathematical Type: Forest (Set of binary non-planar Trees) or Commutative Hopf algebra
Linguistic Description: The computational space or stage of derivation containing the finite multiset of all available syntactic objects (SOs); it acts as the domain for Merge.
Accessible Terms Count: Determined by recursion depth, active heads, or sum of accessible terms of constituent trees
Workspace Size Effect: External Merge increases size by 1 or reduces count by 1; Internal Merge keeps size constant.

Mathematical Type: Magma (free, non-associative, commutative), Sequence /  $n$ -tuple, or Set
Recursive Operation: Binary set formation (Merge), Grafting, or Concatenation
Linguistic Description: Hierarchical structures (binary rooted trees) generated from lexical items and features; the basic building blocks of generative syntax.
Accessible Terms Count: Number of non-root vertices  $\alpha(F)$  or proper nonempty subsets

Mathematical Type: Hopf algebra operator or Binary function
Linguistic Description: The core recursive computational mechanism of the Minimalist Model that builds hierarchical structures from simpler expressions.
The theoretical framework reducing syntax to the simplest possible operations, primarily Merge and Search.

Recursive Operation: Composition of arrows (Dual to directed graph transitivity)
Linguistic Description: Chains of Merge operations acting on workspaces, allowing for the analysis of the generative process path.

Mathematical Type: Hopf algebra (extraction of leading order term in coproduct)
Linguistic Description: An algebraic implementation to eliminate sideward/countercyclic merge by selecting the most efficient path.
Assigns a head or category to a merged set to determine its properties at the interfaces.

Mathematical Type: Quotient map / Section in a moduli space
Linguistic Description: Language-specific constraints that cut down the combinatorial explosion of potential Merge structures.
A referential dependency where the interpretation of an anaphor is determined by a c-commanding antecedent.
The hierarchical representation of how lexical items are combined, defined by dominance and immediate dominance.
